> **Reference only.** The authoritative schema is inline in the corresponding phase file under `skills/positioning-framework/phases/`.
> If this file and the phase file disagree, the phase file wins.

---

# Company Identity Schema (Layer 0)

**Version:** 1.0.0
**Output path:** `.claude/context/company-identity.md`
**Generated by:** `positioning-framework` (Mode 1/2/4), or manually by the user
**Consumed by:** All FunnelEnvy skills

---

## Purpose

This is the foundational context file. Every skill reads it. It contains facts about the company: what it does, who it serves, what it claims, what it can prove, and what constraints apply. No analysis, no competitive comparison, no messaging strategy. Just the raw identity.

A human should be able to fill this out in 15-20 minutes. The positioning-framework skill produces it automatically as part of its L0 output.

---

## Boundary Rules

**IN scope for company-identity.md:**
- Facts the company knows about itself
- Services offered and explicitly NOT offered
- Target market segments and anti-personas
- Stated differentiators (what the company claims, regardless of uniqueness)
- Evidence and proof points (raw, not analyzed for competitive overlap)
- Regulatory and brand constraints
- Pricing model (structure, not competitive positioning)
- Buying triggers (what causes prospects to seek this company)

**OUT of scope (belongs in L1 files):**
- Competitive landscape and battle cards -> `competitive-landscape.md`
- Persona messaging grids, JTBD analysis, value themes, messaging hierarchy, brand voice, tone, language bank -> `audience-messaging.md`
- Positioning scorecard, gap analysis -> `positioning-scorecard.md`

---

## Schema Definition

### YAML Frontmatter

```yaml
---
schema: company-identity
schema_version: "1.0.0"
generated_by: positioning-framework  # or "manual"
last_updated: 2026-02-14
last_updated_by: positioning-framework
confidence: 3  # 1-5, overall confidence in data accuracy

provenance:
  has_client_input: false  # true if any section contains client-origin data
  has_tier0_input: false   # true if any section contains tier-0-origin data
  # Fast-path hints only. The body is always authoritative for section-level provenance.

company:
  name: ""
  url: ""
  founded: ""          # Year
  hq: ""               # City, State or City, Country
  legal_entity: ""     # Optional. Full legal name if different from brand name
  ga4_property: ""     # Optional. GA4 property ID (e.g., "properties/342047065"). Set by positioning-framework --property flag.

category:
  primary: ""          # The market category. Use buyer language, not internal jargon.
  also_known_as: []    # Other category terms buyers use to find companies like this

target_market:
  company_size: ""     # e.g., "Mid-market to upper-mid-market"
  revenue_range: ""    # e.g., "$50M-$5B"
  geo: ""              # e.g., "US national" or "North America"
  segments: []         # e.g., ["PE-backed", "high-growth", "in transition"]
  industries: []       # e.g., ["energy", "healthcare", "financial services"]
---
```

**Field notes:**
- `confidence`: Skills use this to decide whether to trust the data or re-research. Below 3 = treat as draft.

> **Confidence rule:** File-level confidence in frontmatter MUST equal the minimum of all **REQUIRED** section-level confidence scores in the body. OPTIONAL sections (Company Stats, Pricing Model, Glossary, Buying Triggers, Retired Positioning, Category Gap) do not participate in the min() calculation. This is a mechanical calculation, not a judgment.
- `company.ga4_property`: Set automatically when positioning-framework runs with `--property`. Used by ga4-audit for auto-discovery. Value is the full GA4 property identifier (e.g., "properties/342047065").
- `category.primary`: This is what buyers search for, not what the company calls itself. If there's a gap, note it in the body under Category Gap.
- `target_market.segments`: Behavioral segments, not firmographics. "PE-backed" is a segment. "100-500 employees" is a firmographic that goes in `company_size`.

---

### Markdown Body Sections

Every section below is a heading in the markdown body. Sections marked **REQUIRED** must be present for the file to be considered complete. Optional sections can be omitted if data isn't available.

---

#### 1. Company Overview (REQUIRED)

2-4 sentences. What this company does, for whom, and at what scale. Written for someone who has never heard of the company. No marketing language -- just facts.

```markdown
## Company Overview

[Company name] is a [category] serving [target market]. [Key fact about scale/scope].
[One sentence on what makes them notable, if anything obvious].
<!-- origin: research -->
```

**Used by:** Every skill. This is the "who am I talking about?" context.

---

#### 2. Services & Capabilities (REQUIRED)

What the company actually offers. Grouped into logical categories if the raw list is long (more than 6 items). Include the raw list too if it matters for completeness.

```markdown
## Services & Capabilities

### Core Services
- [Service 1]: [One-line description]
- [Service 2]: [One-line description]

### Extended Services
- [Service 3]: [One-line description]

### Technology Partnerships
- [Partner 1], [Partner 2], [Partner 3]
```

**Used by:** Website audit (checks if services are clearly communicated), competitive research (maps service overlap), copy briefs (knows what to write about).

---

#### 3. Service Exclusions (REQUIRED)

What the company explicitly does NOT do. This prevents downstream skills from accidentally positioning into services the company can't deliver. Omitting this section was the #1 gap in early auto-generated output.

```markdown
## Service Exclusions

- **[Excluded service]**: [Why -- e.g., "Regulatory restriction", "Not our model", "Out of scope"]
- **[Excluded service]**: [Why]
<!-- origin: client -->
```

**Used by:** Every skill. Hard constraint. Copy skills must never imply the company offers excluded services. Competitive research must not position against firms in excluded categories.

---

#### 4. Target Segments (REQUIRED)

Expands on the frontmatter `target_market` with behavioral detail. Who are the ideal customers, and what condition are they in when they buy?

```markdown
## Target Segments

| Segment | Description | Buying Condition | Origin |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|--------|
| [Name]  | [Who they are] | [What's happening when they buy] | research |
```

**Used by:** Audience personas (builds full persona profiles from these segments), website audit (checks if the site speaks to these segments), ad copy (targeting criteria).

---

#### 5. Anti-Personas (REQUIRED)

Who the company explicitly does NOT serve. Include why and who's a better fit. This is a hard constraint for all downstream content.

```markdown
## Anti-Personas

| Anti-Persona | Why Not Us | Better Served By | Origin |
|-------------|-----------|-----------------|--------|
| [Who]       | [Reason]  | [Alternative]    | research |
```

**Used by:** Every content skill. Never write copy that would attract anti-personas. Ad targeting skills use this as an exclusion list.

---

#### 6. Stated Differentiators (REQUIRED)

What the company claims makes it different. Listed as-is, without judgment about whether they're actually unique. Uniqueness analysis happens in L1 (`competitive-landscape.md` Claim Overlap Map).

Each differentiator should reference proof points by ID (from the Proof Point Registry below).

```markdown
## Stated Differentiators

| # | Differentiator | Claim | Proof Point IDs | Origin |
|---|---------------|-------|-----------------|--------|
| 1 | [Short name]  | [The specific claim] | P1, P2 | research |
| 2 | [Short name]  | [The specific claim] | P7, P8 | research |
```

**Used by:** Competitive research (tests which claims are actually unique), messaging framework (builds value themes from proven differentiators), website audit (checks if differentiators are visible).

---

#### 7. Proof Point Registry (REQUIRED)

Every piece of evidence the company can cite. This is the single source of truth for proof. All downstream skills reference proof points by ID.

```markdown
## Proof Point Registry

| ID | Type | Content | Source | Strength | Tags | Origin |
|----|------|---------|--------|----------|------|--------|
| P1 | Metric | [Specific metric with numbers] | [URL or source] | [1-5] | [tags] | research |
| P2 | Testimonial | "[Exact quote]" -- [Name, Title, Company] | [URL] | [1-5] | [tags] | research |
| P3 | Case study | [What happened, with quantified outcome if available] | [URL] | [1-5] | [tags] | research |
| P4 | Third-party | [Award, certification, ranking] | [URL] | [1-5] | [tags] | research |
| P5 | Institutional | [Board member, investor, partnership] | [URL] | [1-5] | [tags] | research |
```

**Type values:** Metric, Testimonial, Case study, Third-party, Institutional, Logo

**Tags:** Core tags: `retention`, `culture`, `talent`, `execution`, `cost`, `speed`, `quality`, `enterprise`, `credibility`, `client-voice`, `scale`, `innovation`, `specialization`. Add domain-specific tags as needed. Keep 2-4 tags per proof point.
**Strength scale:**
- 5 = Quantified outcome with third-party verification
- 4 = Specific metric or named testimonial with context
- 3 = Credible but unquantified (logos, awards, general claims)
- 2 = Self-reported without verification
- 1 = Anecdotal or unsubstantiated

**Used by:** Every content and messaging skill. Proof points are referenced by ID (e.g., "back this claim with P1, P4"). Battle cards map proof to objections. Copy briefs map proof to page sections.

---

#### 8. Company Stats (OPTIONAL)

Key numbers about the company. Used for credibility statements and scale arguments.

```markdown
## Company Stats

| Stat | Value | As Of | Source | Origin |
|------|-------|-------|--------|--------|
| Revenue | $149M | 2023 | PR Newswire | research |
| Headcount | 673 | 2024 | Website | research |
| Locations | 26 US markets | 2024 | Website | research |
| YoY Growth | 41% | 2023 | Inc. 5000 | research |
```

**Used by:** Copy briefs (scale proof), objection handling ("you're too small"), competitive battle cards.

---

#### 9. Pricing Model (OPTIONAL)

How the company prices. Not the specific dollar amounts (those rarely go in a context file), but the model and relative positioning. Mark `[NEEDS CONFIRMATION]` if inferred.

```markdown
## Pricing Model

- **Model**: [Hourly / Project-based / Retainer / Subscription / Outcome-based / Hybrid]
- **Relative positioning**: [Budget / Mid-market / Premium / Ultra-premium]
- **Notes**: [Any relevant context -- e.g., "No public pricing. Industry standard is $X-Y/hr."]
<!-- origin: research -->
```

**Used by:** Competitive research (pricing comparison), objection handling ("you're too expensive"), ad targeting (qualify by budget).

---

#### 10. Constraints (REQUIRED)

Hard guardrails on what can and cannot be said. Two categories: regulatory (legal) and brand (self-imposed). Downstream skills treat these as absolute blockers.

```markdown
## Constraints

### Regulatory
- **[Constraint name]**: [What's restricted and why]
  - Restricted terms: [list]
  - Reference: [regulation, governing body, or compliance doc]

### Brand
- **[Constraint name]**: [What's restricted and why]
  - Restricted terms: [list]
  - Context: [why this matters to the brand]
<!-- origin: client -->
```

**Used by:** Every content skill. These are non-negotiable. A copy brief that violates a regulatory constraint is worse than no copy brief at all.

---

#### 11. Glossary (OPTIONAL)

Product names, service names, and terminology that must be used correctly. Include common misspellings or incorrect forms to avoid.

```markdown
## Glossary

| Term | Correct Usage | Incorrect / Avoid | Origin |
|------|--------------|-------------------|--------|
| [Term] | [How to use it] | [What not to say] | research |
```

**Used by:** Every content skill. Prevents downstream copy from using wrong product names or deprecated terminology.

---

#### 12. Buying Triggers (OPTIONAL)

What events cause prospects to seek this company. These are the "moments" that create demand. Different from persona pain points (which are chronic). Triggers are acute events.

```markdown
## Buying Triggers

- [Trigger event]: [Why it creates urgency]
- [Trigger event]: [Why it creates urgency]
<!-- origin: research -->
```

**Used by:** Ad targeting (event-based campaigns), content strategy (trigger-specific content), email sequences (trigger-based nurture).

---

#### 13. Retired Positioning (OPTIONAL)

Past value props, taglines, or campaigns the company has moved away from. This is a constraint: downstream skills should not accidentally re-propose retired messaging.

```markdown
## Retired Positioning

| What | When Used | Why Retired | Origin |
|------|-----------|------------|--------|
| "[Past tagline or value prop]" | [Approximate timeframe] | [Why they stopped using it] | client |
```

**Used by:** Every content skill. Prevents proposing messaging the company has already tried and abandoned.

---

#### 14. Category Gap (OPTIONAL)

If the company's self-described category doesn't match how buyers search, document the gap here. This is a fact (the gap exists), not a recommendation (what to do about it). Recommendations go in L1.

```markdown
## Category Gap

- **Company says**: "[Their self-described category]"
- **Buyers search for**: "[Terms buyers actually use]"
- **Gap**: [Brief description of the mismatch]
<!-- origin: research -->
```

**Used by:** SEO audit, website audit, positioning scorecard.

---

#### 15. Homepage Messaging (REQUIRED)

Exact copy extracted from the homepage hero and top landing pages, with source attribution tags. The H1 must come from the main content area of the page, NOT from navigation menus, mega-menu dropdowns, or header taglines.

```markdown
## Homepage Messaging

- H1: "[exact headline text from main content hero]" [source: website-confirmed]
- Additional H1s: "[if carousel/slider]" / "[second slide]" [source: website-extracted]
- Subhead: "[exact subhead text]" [source: website-confirmed]
- CTA: "[button text]" / "[button text]" [source: website-confirmed]
- Format: [Static / Carousel (N slides) / Video / etc.] [source: user-confirmed]
- Nav taglines (reference only): "[any positioning-sounding taglines found in navigation]"

### Top Landing Pages

| Page URL | H1 | Source |
|----------|----|--------|
| [url]    | "[H1 text]" | website-confirmed |
| [url]    | "[H1 text]" | website-confirmed |
| [url]    | "[H1 text]" | website-confirmed |
```

**Source attribution tags:**
- `website-extracted`: WebFetch returned this content directly
- `website-confirmed`: WebFetch extracted it AND user confirmed it matches
- `user-confirmed`: User provided or corrected this content
- `meta-derived`: Pulled from meta/og tags, not visible page content
- `not-extracted`: JS-rendered, content could not be obtained. Treat as a gap -- never quote `not-extracted` copy as a baseline in experiments or recommendations.

**Purpose:** Consumed by Agent 3 Content Integrity Check and Orchestrator Copy Verification Checkpoint (SKILL.md step 6.5). Provides the experiment baseline for any CRO work on hero copy.

**Used by:** Orchestrator copy verification checkpoint, render-default-deliverables (experiment roadmap baseline), website-audit.

---

## Completeness Checklist

> A checklist item passes with either (a) populated content citing sources or (b) an explicit gap marker. Silent omission fails. Honest gaps pass.

A company-identity.md file is considered **complete** when:

- [ ] YAML frontmatter has all required fields (company, category, target_market)
- [ ] Company Overview is filled (2-4 sentences)
- [ ] Services & Capabilities lists at least the core services
- [ ] Service Exclusions is present (even if empty with a note: "None identified")
- [ ] Target Segments has entries from verified sources (target: 2+; fewer is acceptable with gap marker)
- [ ] Anti-Personas has entries from verified sources (target: 1+; fewer is acceptable with gap marker)
- [ ] Stated Differentiators has entries with proof point references (target: 2+; fewer is acceptable with gap marker)
- [ ] Proof Point Registry has entries from verified sources (target: 5+; fewer is acceptable with gap marker)
- [ ] Constraints section is present (even if no regulatory constraints exist)
- [ ] `confidence` score is set and honest

A file missing REQUIRED sections should have `confidence: 1` in the frontmatter and a note about what's missing.

---

## Versioning

When updating an existing company-identity.md:
- Bump `last_updated`
- Update `last_updated_by` to the extending skill name
- Add a changelog entry at the bottom of the file
- If proof points are added/removed, re-check all `Proof Point IDs` references in Stated Differentiators
- If services change, check Service Exclusions still holds

```markdown
## Changelog

| Date | Change | By |
|------|--------|----|
| 2026-02-14 | Initial creation | positioning-framework |
| 2026-03-01 | Added regulatory constraints per client review | manual |
```
